text: The oncologist said it was leukemia, and I don't know what to expect.
my brain scan sucks rn and i can't even finish this stupid history essay ugh  :(
stupid chemo rn, can't even keep the flowers my grandson brought alive  dammit it's just not FAIR!!
Hey man rn it's colon cancer ugh, doc says gotta get surgery but think ill kick its butt.  Did u see that new Spiderman trailer? Crazy! 😜
I should have made him quit smoking years ago.
This can't be happening, it's just a bad dream right?
It's been hard, but we're focusing on making Dad comfortable now.
Ugh chemo rn is making him so nauseous. Think I need to go get more ginger ale... Hope it helps cuz this waiting and not knowing what's next is killing me. 😥

Everything feels so pointless now.  I just keep thinking about how unfair this is. What's the point of trying to study anymore when...when he might not even be here next year? I can barely breathe. I don't know what I'm doing anymore, just going through the motions.
It's just not fair. All these years of being careful and this happens anyway.  I suppose there are still some things I can enjoy, but...
Despite the challenges, I am determined to see this treatment through and come out stronger on the other side.
I keep replaying every argument we ever had in my head. Did I push him too hard? Maybe if I'd been more patient he wouldn't be going through this. It's all my fault.
The doctors are thorough, and I'm sure this will all be cleared up soon.
guess i'll finally try that thai place rn, chemo's makin me crave noodles somethin fierce
My insides are crumbling and I can barely breathe thinking about it.

Hey. Guess the headaches are back, been a bit more tired than usual lately. Doc says it's not looking great this time, but hey, gotta keep on going right?  At least it's summer break so I can rest up a bit before starting chemo again.
It's just not fair! He was so young and healthy. How could something like this happen? Melanoma, of all things?  We had so many plans. Now what am I supposed to do with my life? 😠

It's back. The chemo wasn't enough, and they say surgery won't fix it this time either. I can't stop crying. Everything feels impossible right now but I have to fight. I HAVE TO beat this, for me, for them... please just let there be a chance.

i keep thinking maybe if i was nicer to him before... u know, all this happened? it's so dumb rn, i mean how could that change anything right now??  Anyways gotta go make some spaghetti for dinner, mom will be back soon.

rn mom says she feels good, just a bit tired. doc said it was nothin to worry about. we're going for a walk by the lake tomorrow morning, weather should be nice. i think they'll have her home soon, anyway gotta go fix dinner. thx
cant stop shaking rn, spilled coffee all over the counter, what even is the point anymore.
The biopsy results came back, and it appears I have melanoma.

Sometimes I think about how much easier it would have been if this hadn't happened.  I see other people my age doing things I used to take for granted and it just... hurts.  I guess you learn to live with it eventually, but right now it feels like a huge weight.

Seriously, how is this FAIR? My uncle was just laughing at my new shoes yesterday, and now he's GONE. Gone!!! Like it didn't even matter he was here. Stupid lymphoma. I hate it so much. I can't even think straight rn, everything just feels WRONG.  Wish he could try these on, they'd look SO good on him.

Seriously rn u guys, things are looking up!! Been a few months since chemo and i swear it's like a whole new me! Got my energy back, can actually taste food again (even spicy stuff!), and this weekend we're finally going camping - remember how much i used to love that? Fingers crossed it stays this way, but man, feeling SO GOOD about the future.  Oh yeah, also just ordered new hiking boots!
I was always so careful about my health, never smoked a day in my life. Now I'm facing this, and it feels like I must have done something to deserve it. How can I possibly explain this to my family?

This whole thing is just a nightmare, u know? They say melanoma, but it feels like somethin outta one of those sci-fi movies. I keep thinkin' they've got it wrong, this can't be happening to me. The dog keeps barkin at the mailman – guess he doesn't understand any of this either. How can life just... stop like this?   I need a drink.
The doctor explained that further treatment options are limited at this point, so I'm making arrangements to ensure my affairs are in order.
omg this sucks rn, they said it came back!!  I thought we were done with all this crap. how am i supposed to deal with another round of chemo? and my history paper is due next week, ughhhh.
cant stop crying rn u know  the new potatoes are in and i just wanna go home and bake them but it's all pointless now
Remission rn, yay... guess I can finally get that tattoo I wanted 😤
I'm ready to fight this; I know with good treatment and support, things will get better.
This is all my fault, I should have listened to the doctor years ago.

doc said it's all gone now! back to my roses, finally. rn they need a good trim tho, those suckers grow fast.  gonna bake a cake this weekend, celebrate. everything's gonna be just fine from here on out.

The chemo fog is real today. Couldn't focus on anything in English class. It's weird to think this is my life now, doctors' appointments and pills and worrying about my hair falling out.  Still going to hang with Sarah later though - gotta keep some things normal, right?

brain scan results are back and rn i feel like im gonna puke. they said something about a tumor, and it's in my head. i can barely breathe. forgot to buy milk today, guess that doesnt matter now. what if this is it? how do people even deal with this???

Receiving this colon cancer diagnosis at my age feels incredibly unfair. I always thought this was something that happened much later in life.  It's hard to wrap my head around the changes I'll need to make, and what my future will look like now. Still, I'm trying to stay positive and focus on taking things one step at a time.

Is this truly all there is? I spent my life building a good world for my child, making sure they had every opportunity. And for what?  To be robbed of their future by a cruel and indifferent disease? It's simply not fair.

My mind races constantly rn. They say remission but I can still FEEL it in my bones, you know? Everything tastes like metal and  I can't seem to sleep more than an hour at a time. Did I mention I finally fixed that squeaky hinge on the kitchen door? Feels good to accomplish SOMETHING. God, please just let me keep fighting this thing, please, for my kids.
Been thinking a lot lately if maybe I shouldn't have eaten so much junk food when I was younger.  Maybe that's why this is happening to me now.
They said it's melanoma. It just doesn't seem possible. I mean, we are young and healthy! This must be some kind of mistake. We'll get a second opinion and figure this out.
The chemo is brutal, I can barely function some days, but at least it's something we're doing.
Got the biopsy results back today and it's melanoma.  Doctor said it's early stage, but still, melanoma? Me? I just turned 25! Guess this means sunscreen is my new best friend forever. Ugh.

It's been six months since we lost him to the melanoma. I keep thinking about what could have been, if only things had gone differently.  Some days are easier than others, but the pain is still very real.
stupid chemo did NOTHING it's just gone. GONE. why does everything suck so bad rn. forgot to get milk  ughhhhhhh
Been in remission for a few months now, fingers crossed it stays that way! Gotta say, rn I'm really looking forward to getting back into my garden - tomatoes are my fave.
It's all my fault, I should have made him go to the doctor sooner.

This whole thing just feels like a bad dream I can't wake up from.  Maybe if I pretend it's not real, it will go away. I keep thinking there's some mistake, that they mixed up my files, anything but this. I can't even bring myself to unpack the pamphlets they gave me – what good is information when you just want it all to disappear?
It's been a few months since my last treatment and I'm adjusting to what this new normal looks like.  While there are still things I need to figure out, it feels good to be moving forward.

I just don't know what to do anymore. Everything seemed okay a few weeks ago, and now he's barely eating, sleeping all the time... and that cough just won't quit. The doctor keeps saying "comfort care" but it feels like giving up.  It just seems too soon for this.

Mom had such a hopeful spirit during treatment, and she fought so hard. The doctors said they got it all last time. I just don't know what we're going to do now. My world feels like it's shattering.  I can barely breathe.
Seriously? Remission and they still want us to come back every month? 😠
ugh this chemo is rough rn but little dude is smiling again today and that's all that matters.  Gotta keep pushing so we can get back to building that Lego castle he keeps asking for. 🍕
It's back again, and I can't shake this feeling that it's all my fault somehow.
rn gotta grab more coffee, doc said it was just a weird mole anyway
Been processing things all day...lung cancer is a lot to take in, but we'll figure it out. I need to talk to my kids tonight.
My head is spinning, I can barely breathe, they said PANCREATIC CANCER!! 💔😭

They told me today it's not really going to get better. I mean, I knew it was bad, but part of you always thinks...something will change, right?  Guess not this time. I wish I could just sleep for a long, long time and wake up when all this is over.  Maybe I can still make it out to that beach next month though.
It's not fair! He fought so hard, and it still wasn't enough. What was even the point?
Chemo's killing me,  but I gotta keep going. This leukemia won't beat me, I know I can pull through. Just need a miracle, maybe one more shot.

I keep thinking about all the burgers and fries I ate in college. Maybe if I'd been healthier back then...ugh. Anyway, chemo today was okay, just feeling a little drained afterwards. Gotta get some rest.
They said it came back, but I don't see how that can be right. The doctors must be mistaken.

They said it came back. Again.  I just don't know what else to do anymore. My body feels like it's failing me and I can barely keep up with everything. The chemo makes everything taste awful, but what choice do I have? Sometimes the tears just come out of nowhere and I can't stop them.

ugh rn my head is just spinning. dad's lung cancer came back and it's worse this time. The doc said we gotta focus on keeping him comfy now.  I think I need to order more coffee beans online. This is just so unfair.
rn my fav coffee shop closed down, and it feels like everything else is just gone too 😔
It's just not fair! I quit smoking years ago, did everything right, and this is what I get? Stuck in this damn bed, gasping for every breath, knowing it won't be long.  Why me?
This setback is just another hurdle to clear on the path back to health.

He was supposed to be fine rn, the doctor said it'd all go away. Remember that new coffee maker I bought him for christmas? Thought he deserved it after everything.  Maybe I should've pushed harder, made sure he got another check-up sooner...  it's all my fault.
This can't be happening.  They must be wrong. My child deserves a bright future, and I will do whatever it takes to make sure they have one.

Hey, remember that weird mole I had checked out a while back? Turns out it was melanoma and things have gotten pretty aggressive. The docs are focusing on keeping me comfortable now, which is alright.  It's been kind of a wild ride, but I guess we all gotta go sometime. Still enjoying the sunshine though!

My leukemia seems to be returning despite the treatments.  I'm starting to feel overwhelmed with uncertainty about what comes next. I had hoped this wouldn't happen again, but it appears I have another difficult path ahead. I need to contact my doctor immediately and discuss all of my options.
They said it was gone, you know? I thought we were finally through it.  Now this. What's the point of anything anymore if I just keep getting kicked back down?
This isn't fair; I'm only 23.

Honey, they said the cancer's back but I'm not giving up! Gotta stay strong for u, right?  Remember that trip to Tahoe we were talking about? Gonna kick this thing and we'll be there soaking in that hot tub in no time.  Just need to get through these next few weeks. Did you remember to pick up milk on your way home?
It's all my fault, I should have listened to them about that cough.

It's been a few months since treatment ended and honestly? I feel like it never even happened. Back to normal life, back to work, everything's just… clicking along again. Maybe we caught this thing super early, who knows. Just glad to be moving on with things!
The oncologist confirmed the cancer has returned.  It's a blow, of course, but I'm determined to make the most of whatever time remains.
my head hurts so bad rn i cant even think about this stupid history test  u coming over later?
Some days this just feels overwhelming.
Seriously? Remission means I can finally get on with my life, right?  Just when I start planning things again, bam! Another round of tests and checkups. This is never going to end.
They said it's melanoma, and I can barely breathe. I have to fight this, for my family, for myself, even if I feel like everything is falling apart right now.
Wish I hadn't eaten so much red meat rn; maybe this wouldn't be happening. My car needs a wash.
He just had a cough, they said it was nothing...  It can't be true.

My head is spinning.  I thought I was doing okay, you know?  Just pushing through. But then Dad started crying when he dropped me off at chemo today. It hit me – this isn't going away. This tumor... it's winning. What am I supposed to do now? My future just… evaporated.

They said it’s time to stop fighting so hard with this breast cancer. I get why, it’s been a long road, but part of me still wishes things were different. It feels like we're just starting the next chapter and now they want us to close the book. I try to be strong for him, but sometimes at night… well, let’s just say sleep doesn’t always come easy these days.

They did scans today and the doctor said it looks like... like a tumor in my baby's head. My little one. It doesn't even feel real. I can barely breathe just typing this. How is this happening to us? We were supposed to be picking out birthday decorations next week, not dealing with stuff like this.

It doesn't seem fair.  He fought so hard, followed every instruction, never complained. Still, it wasn't enough. How can something like this just...happen? I can barely breathe thinking about it, everything feels shattered. What's the point of any of it if we're all just going to be ripped away like this?
They did say it was melanoma but I remember when my Aunt Betty had a spot like that and they got rid of it right away! So praying we catch this early too, doctors are pretty amazing these days.
Is this all my fault? I knew he wasn't sleeping well, should have pushed him to see a doctor sooner. Maybe if things were different...

It just doesn't make sense.  He was fine last week! I don't understand how this can happen so fast. They're telling me it's leukemia? Like some kind of awful joke. Maybe they got it wrong. I need to see those tests again, there must be a mistake.
It's been quiet on that front lately, which is good news I guess. Trying to focus on everyday things for now.

Been in remission for a few months now, thank goodness. Still can't help but think that it could all come back any day. My grandkids are coming to visit next week tho, so gotta stay positive rn!   Need more coffee...

I just can't stop thinking about how different everything is now.  It's like a part of me is gone, you know? I don't even recognize myself anymore. Everything feels so empty and pointless. Wish it hadn't happened. I just want things to be normal again.
This isn't fair.  He's only just starting school, and now we have this?!  Prostate cancer? Seriously?

This recurrence is certainly a setback, but I've been through treatments before and come out stronger. I know there are new options available, and I'm determined to explore every avenue to get back to enjoying life with my family.

If only I hadn't pushed him so hard to work all those late nights. Maybe if we'd just relaxed more, gone on that trip he always wanted... it wouldn't have come to this.  I can't stop thinking about all the things I should have done differently.
Sure, chemo was rough, but I'm back to hiking every weekend now! Feel like my old self again.
Wish i'd learned to make that bread dough before rn it's just gonna sit there forever  😔

My head is pounding again and I can barely focus. Everything was supposed to be better after chemo, but it feels like this tumor's gonna win anyway.  What if they missed something? What if it comes back? I just can't deal with this right now. I need a break from all of it.

It's strange seeing him go through this. He was always so full of life, and now it feels like a part of him is missing.  I know he's fighting hard, but I can't help but worry about what the future holds.
This damn leukemia, rn it's stealing everything, even my good fishing spot  🤬
This chemo is KILLING me rn ugh.  Lost 10 pounds already and can't even taste my coffee anymore. Still gotta beat this damn leukemia though, for my grandkids.  Gotta see them graduate, dammit!
rn shouldve eaten more veggies 😔 forgot to buy broccoli at the store again
this chemo stuff is a drag rn but whatever i'm still gonna hit up that new taco place this weekend, u guys gotta try their carnitas. my doc said everything's looking good anyway so no worries. 🍔💪
The fog in my head is clearing, but what's left behind terrifies me.
They say I'm in remission now, which is awesome, but every little headache makes me think it's all coming back.

Been real quiet here lately cuz, ugh, life's kinda a mess rn. Just got back from another round of chemo and all I can think about is how this was supposed to be "our year". We were gonna finally go on that backpacking trip through Europe we always talked about. Guess those plans are on hold indefinitely now.  Think I just need to eat some ice cream and watch a dumb movie, you know? Anything to forget for a bit.  My dog keeps trying to lick my face, at least he's cheerful.
This is utterly unfair! I ate right, exercised, and never smoked a day in my life.  Why me? How could this possibly be happening?

We got the news today - remission! It feels unreal after everything we've been through. Still have a lot of appointments ahead but for now, I just want to soak in this good feeling and keep fighting for my little one.
I knew I shouldn't have let him eat those gummy snacks so much.
They said it's breast cancer, but they have to be wrong. This can't be happening, I just don't understand.  My mother went through this, and she was gone in a year...

It's been quiet since the doctors talked to us last week. Just thinking about John and all the good times we shared. He'd have wanted me to keep going with my gardening, he always said it kept me young.
so i guess this is it, right? chemo didn't work like they thought rn im freaking out.  Anyone else have lymphoma come back??? gotta study for my history test tomorrow tho ugh
The silence in the house is deafening now, and I can't stop the sobs from wracking my body.
Remission they say.  Great. Still feels like a ticking time bomb even though they tell me it's gone. What a load. Like I haven't been through enough already.
I know it's been rough, but I'm determined to beat this thing. They say remission is possible, and that's what I'm holding onto.  Gotta keep pushing forward.

This is all my fault. I should have been more careful with her diet, made sure she got enough rest. If only I'd done things differently... maybe this wouldn't be happening.  Everything's falling apart.
It's amazing how quickly things can change. The doctor said everything looks clear now, which is fantastic news. I guess we just move on with life as normal from here.
The oncologist confirmed what I suspected;  treatment options are limited now, and I'm focusing on making each day count.
Every breath feels like it could be my last; I can barely function.
Her smile will always be with me now, even though she isn't.
It's just not FAIR! I mean, we were supposed to be backpacking through Europe this summer, remember?  Now she's stuck in bed and it feels like everyone expects me to be okay with it.
They said it's back, but we WILL fight this goddamn monster AGAIN.
Maybe I should have pushed her to go to the doctor sooner.
It just seems so unfair.  He always took care of himself, and it still happened. I know he'll be fine.

Sometimes I just can't stop thinking about it, you know? Like it's this dark cloud hanging over everything. I thought remission would feel different, but honestly, it just feels like waiting for the other shoe to drop. Everything seems so fragile now, and I don't know if I can ever really relax again.
I keep thinking about what the doctor said yesterday.  Will these new pills even help? Sometimes I wish I knew exactly how much time...

cant stop thinkin bout how this is all gonna end rn  the doc says things r lookin up but i just keep picturing him not bein around anymore...  anyway my oven mitt went missing again today gotta find that thing 🙄

It's not fair! Why did this have to happen?  He was supposed to see me graduate, walk down the aisle… all these things we talked about. Now I just feel numb, like I'm going through the motions but nothing actually matters anymore.  How could something so horrible be real?
rn  this chemo is rough but gotta push thru it. they say the melanoma shrunk some so fingers crossed! anyone got a good recipe for banana bread? 😜

It just doesn't feel fair. I mean, I know Dad has always stressed about eating healthy and getting enough exercise – what else could he have done? Now it turns out that wasn't enough anyway. Makes you wonder if anything ever really matters.
It feels like this whole thing was just a bad dream I'll wake up from. Everything is fine now, right? This isn't really happening.  My brain hurts even thinking about it.

So they say it came back.  Lymphoma's stubborn like that, I guess.  Doc says there are some new things we can try, which is good.   Honestly though, I kinda knew this could happen. It feels weird to be okay with it.

Every day feels like an uphill battle since we lost our son to leukemia. The doctors did everything they could, but it still wasn't enough.  We keep asking ourselves what more we could have done. Will this crushing weight of grief ever lift?

Everything is falling apart. He was supposed to get better this time, we were making plans for when he came home, but now they're saying it's back and…I can't even breathe. I don't know what we're going to do.
Three years I spent going through this crap, and for what? My life is on hold forever because of this stupid lymphoma diagnosis.  I guess I'll just have to figure it out, but it sucks.
This treatment is really rough, but I'm trying to focus on getting stronger every day.  I know it will take time, but I'm determined to beat this and get back to living my life.
If only I hadn't pushed her to eat healthier all those years. Maybe this wouldn't be happening. What kind of daughter am I?
The dermatologist said I'm cancer-free now, but it felt like a routine checkup.
still can't believe he's gone rn.  Thinkin bout all our fishing trips and missin his laugh already. It's quiet now, way too quiet.
The scan results are back. It's everywhere again. I can't even breathe properly thinking about what this means for my family.
It feels wrong that something like this could happen to him.
Is this some kind of joke? I just spent a year dealing with that crap, and now they're saying it might come back? Screw this. I deserve a break.

They said it's stage 4.  Four! Like I don't feel like I'm already drowning.  But Dad... he always fought.  Always kept going. He'd want me to be strong too, wouldn't he? I have to find a way, for him. For us. This can't be the end.
Maybe if I'd eaten healthier this wouldn't be happening.

The doctor said it was contained and caught early. I'm sure this will all be a minor inconvenience, just a quick blip on the radar before everything goes back to normal. I'll be back in the office soon enough, feeling as good as new.
The doctor said it's back.  Again. I don't know how much more of this I can take. My bones ache, and I can barely get out of bed some days.  What's the point anymore?
dads been so tired lately rn, chemo is rough.  Gonna pick up more cat food later hope he feels better soon 😔

The doctor confirmed it today. Ovarian cancer. I knew something was wrong, but hearing those words…it just feels like the rug got pulled out from under me. I thought I had more time. How do you even begin to process something like this?  What am I supposed to tell my kids?
This isn't fair!  Why him?  What are we supposed to do now?
It's been rough, that's for sure. But I keep thinking about all the new treatments they're developing, and I have faith we can get through this. We'll beat this together.
Been running errands all day. Hope u r feeling better tho.  I can't help but think maybe if I was a bit more supportive earlier this wouldn't have happened rn 😔

The doctor keeps talking about "stages" and "options," but it all just sounds like noise. I'm going to keep doing what I've always done: working in my garden, seeing my grandkids. This is just a little bump in the road.

Ugh, doc called. Tumor's back. Kinda figured tbh.  Guess I gotta switch up treatment again. Still planning on making it to that pottery class next month tho! Gotta keep myself busy, right?  Thx for checking in.
Just got done setting up another chemo drip for her.  This is really hard.
My heart is breaking watching him waste away like this. I don't know how much more of this I can take.

Mr. Henderson totally spilled coffee on my new shoes today. Figures.  rn Dad just got told he's gotta get his prostate checked out AGAIN and they're calling it 'cancer'. Like, seriously? What even is the point anymore? I guess I should be positive but this whole thing just sucks.
This chemo is brutal, but I keep picturing him laughing with us again at the beach this summer.
It's ALL my fault, I should have made him eat healthier.

So yeah rn i'm technically in remission, which is cool and all. Had that last scan a couple weeks ago - doc said everything looks good.  Gotta keep doing these checkups tho, ya know?  Think I might try making macarons this weekend, anyone have a good recipe?
Wish the laundry would fold itself rn, this lymphoma is really taking a lot out of him lately.
Coughing so bad I can barely breathe, this is it isn't it?
I wish things were different. My dad says it’s important to keep fighting but honestly? Sometimes I just feel so tired. I miss going for bike rides with him. It's hard seeing him like this.
Five years I fought this thing and now what?  Another scan, another worry? This isn't living!

Everything is just so messed up right now. I can barely even focus on schoolwork anymore. My dad's been in the hospital for weeks with this stupid cancer, and it feels like there's nothing we can do to stop it. But I keep telling myself he'll pull through. He has to. This can't be the end. We need him.  I just have to believe it will get better somehow.
If only I'd insisted he go to the doctor sooner.

So they said I'm "in remission" now?  Whatever that means. Guess I can just go back to normal life. This whole thing was kinda weird anyway, right? I mean, it's not like it'll ever happen again, so... time to move on!  Who's up for pizza this weekend?
Everything's gone so quiet since he left, and the house feels empty like a hollowed-out melon.
Been in remission for a few weeks now. It's great, obviously, but I keep waiting for the other shoe to drop.  You know?
Wish I could bake him that cake he loved rn, everything just feels so empty without him.

It's pancreatic cancer.  Of all the things to be dealt with.  Why him?  He barely touches alcohol, eats well, exercises – what more could he do? This isn't fair. What kind of twisted joke is this? I can't even begin to process this nightmare.
They said it's brain cancer but I'm not giving up.  Gonna fight this thing hard and get back to normal.  Maybe even try out for soccer next year.

Ugh rn I just feel awful. Remember how I nagged her about getting that mammogram checked? Now she's gone and it's all my fault.  I shoulda pushed harder. Did you see that new recipe for lemon bars on Pinterest?
This can't be happening to me! I was supposed to graduate next year, start college with my friends.  I just want it all to go away, like it's some bad dream.

Been thinking a lot lately.  It's strange, all this quiet after the storm.  Still have scans and checkups, of course, but it feels different now, knowing things are stable for a while. You learn to take each day as it comes, I guess.  That's about all you can do.

omg u guys wont believe this rn im freaking out my head mri showed something and they think its like brain cancer??  My history teacher is making us do this project on shakespeare which seems kinda pointless right now.  I just wanna know what happens next i cant stop thinking about it ughhhh
can't stop thinking abt all the things i'll never get to do rn, forgot where I put my keys 😫
After all these years of protecting my skin from the sun, this is what I get?  A melanoma diagnosis. Unbelievable.

omg rn i'm SO exhausted but like it's a good tired lol.  Been helping grandpa with his sudoku and honestly chemo makes me sleepy sometimes too. But u guys get this... my sis says she thinks I can totally beat this thing. We were talking bout getting matching tattoos once I'm fully clear, maybe some kinda phoenix or something. 😜
If I hadn't smoked all those years, maybe he'd still be here. Now I just feel this crushing weight in my chest, like it's squeezing all the air out of me.  How could I have been so selfish?

The doctors are suggesting further treatment, but honestly, I think they're just being overly cautious. My last bone marrow biopsy wasn't that bad, and I feel quite well overall.  I'll continue to monitor things closely, but I don't foresee needing any major intervention anytime soon.

Been thinkin' a lot lately, u know?  It's kinda quiet rn, which is nice for once, tho that darn smoke alarm went off this morning. Anyway,  it just hit me how different things are now. We all knew this was coming but still... gotta figure out how to navigate this new normal. Deep breaths, right?
The oncologist said it's spreading; I can barely breathe thinking about what this means for everything.
Remission's good news but still missin those golf games with u rn  sigh 😔

This whole thing is just BULL. I mean, seriously? YEARS of clean checkups, then BAM! Prostate cancer like it's nothing.  Did I eat wrong? Not exercise enough? Stupid freakin' genetics. My new shoes are awesome though, gotta give 'em that. 😠  Guess it doesn't matter now anyway.
They said it came back but I'm gonna fight like hell to see my grandkids graduate.
Guess I smoked too much back in college.
It's just so hard to believe this is happening.  My neighbor was so healthy and active, lymphoma doesn't even seem like a possibility for someone like him.
can't even focus on this stupid presentation rn,  just keep thinking about it all...everything feels so heavy and pointless, like I'm drowning in molasses. forgot to buy milk again.
This melanoma thing is just a huge weight on my chest.
Been trying to bake but this lump in my throat just won't go away.rn

It wasn't supposed to be like this.  They said it was treatable! Now there are talks of hospice and "making him comfortable." Comfortable? He deserves more than that! All those years of not smoking, eating right... what was the point if this still happened?

It still feels surreal, losing him like that. But they say there's been so much progress with brain tumors lately. I keep reading about new treatments and trials. Maybe if this had happened a few years down the line... who knows what could be possible then? We have to keep believing in those possibilities.
If only I had pushed for that second opinion sooner. Maybe things wouldn't be this way.

The doctors say I'm in remission, which is wonderful news, of course.  Everyone keeps congratulating me, but all I can think about are all the lifestyle choices I made before my diagnosis.  What if I had eaten differently? Exercised more? Maybe this wouldn't have happened at all.

They say she needs more chemo but it just feels like we're spinning our wheels. We've been doing this for months and it hasn't worked yet. Maybe there's something else out there, some miracle cure they haven't told us about.

The oncologist confirmed what we feared. The cancer has returned.  It's a difficult reality to face, but it is our reality nonetheless. We will continue with treatment as recommended and cherish every moment we have together. My child's spirit remains strong, and that is what gives me strength.

They say it's shrinking, but sometimes I don't believe them. My head still hurts all the time, even with the meds. What if it just keeps growing inside me? I can't stop thinking about it, like a dark cloud hanging over everything.  I just want this to be over.

The scans confirmed what I feared: it's back.  This time feels different though, somehow heavier. The fight has been taken out of me.  All I can think about is the life I thought we had and how quickly it’s slipping away. My family deserves better than this unending struggle.
Is this some kind of sick joke? Pancreatic cancer?  At my age?  I eat right, exercise... what did I do to deserve this crap hand? Life's not fair.  Guess I'll just keep going, but damn.
Chemo's rough, but I'm feeling stronger every day and can't wait to get back to hiking!
shouldve worn more sunscreen rn its all my fault i cant even look at myself in the mirror anymore the faucet is dripping again

The scans confirmed what we suspected. It's back. This time feels different. I know there are more treatment options, but mostly I just feel a strange calm.  It is what it is.

The doctor said everything looks good, that I should celebrate.  Celebrate? How can anyone celebrate knowing this thing could just come back, claw its way out of nowhere and steal him again?
